title: Low-Rank Matrix Sensing (compressive matrix recovery)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD contains the widefield-fluorescence template (genesis fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in the canonical matrix sensing / low-rank recovery literature (Recht-Fazel-Parrilo 2010, Candes-Plan 2011).

E.description: Recover a low-rank matrix X in R^{n1 x n2} of rank r from m linear measurements y_k = <A_k, X> + n_k; measurement operators A_k are typically drawn from i.i.d. Gaussian/Bernoulli ensembles or structured (partial Fourier, matrix completion patterns). Specializes to matrix completion when A_k are canonical basis matrices e_{i_k} e_{j_k}^T (single-entry observation).
E.forward_model: y_k = <A_k, X> + n_k,  k = 1..m,  X = U * V^T,  rank(X) = r
E.world_state_x: Matrix X in R^{n1 x n2} with rank r << min(n1, n2); e.g., phase-retrieval intensity matrix, video cube reshape, recommender ratings.
E.observation_y: m scalar linear measurements y in R^m (Gaussian/Bernoulli observation noise).
physical_parameters_theta: m: number of measurements (typical regime m = O(r * (n1 + n2)))
measurement ensemble type (Gaussian, Bernoulli, partial_Fourier, entry_sampling)

W.regime: Recovery guaranteed w.h.p. when m >= C * r * (n1 + n2) and A satisfies matrix-RIP; nuclear-norm minimization is exact for noiseless case; stability bound ||X_hat - X||_F <= C * sigma * sqrt(m) / smallest_singular_value.

C.solver_class: nuclear-norm minimization (SVT, FPC), Burer-Monteiro factorization (alternating GD, ProcGenRAM), Riemannian optimization on fixed-rank manifolds
C.error_bound: ||X_hat - X||_F <= C1 * sigma * sqrt(r * (n1+n2)) / sqrt(m) + C2 * ||X - X_r||_* (best rank-r approx)
C.complexity: O(m * n1 * n2) per iteration for dense; O(r * (n1+n2)) per iteration for factorized Burer-Monteiro
